Google Drive uses OAuth and you must authenticate and link your Google account via  [dashboard](https://dashboard.shotstack.io/integrations/google-drive), not in the request.

provider.description: The destination to send assets to - set to `google-drive` for Google Drive.

options.description: Additional Google Drive configuration and features.

GoogleDriveDestinationOptions.description: Pass the folder ID and options to configure how assets are stored in Google Drive.

folderId.description: The Google Drive folder ID where asset will be stored. The folder ID is required and can be retrieved from the URL when logged in to Google Drive, e.g. https://drive.google.com/drive/u/0/folders/1r-eTY6OLO8tzQRKwMyq-fIrQ_7AJEI6A.

filename.description: Use your own filename instead of the default filenames generated by Shotstack. Note: omit the file extension as this will be appended depending on the output format. Also `-poster.jpg` and `-thumb.jpg` will be appended for poster and thumbnail images.

VimeoDestination.description: Send videos to [Vimeo](https://shotstack.io/docs/guide/serving-assets/destinations/vimeo/) video hosting and streaming service. Vimeo credentials are required and added via the [dashboard](https://dashboard.shotstack.io/integrations/vimeo), not in the request.

provider.description: The destination to send video to - set to `vimeo` for Vimeo.

VimeoDestinationOptions.description: Pass additional options to control how Vimeo publishes video, including name, description and privacy settings.

name.description: A name or title for the video that will be displayed on the Vimeo website.

description.description: A description of the video that will be displayed on the Vimeo website.

privacy.description: Options to control the visibility of videos and privacy features.

folderUri.description: The Vimeo folder URI to upload the video to. The folder must already exist in your Vimeo account.

VimeoDestinationPrivacyOptions.description: Options to control the visibility of videos and privacy features.

view.description:
Set who can view the videos. Available options are:
  • `anybody` - Anyone can view the video.
  • `nobody` - Only the video owner can view the video.
  • `contacts` - Only contacts can view the video.
  • `password` - A password is required to view the video.
  • `unlisted` - The video is not listed on Vimeo.

embed.description:
Set who can embed the video. Available options are:
  • `public` - Anyone can embed the video.
  • `private` - Only the video owner can embed the video.
  • `whitelist` - Only whitelisted domains can embed the video.

comments.description:
Set who can comment on the video. Available options are:
  • `anybody` - Anyone can comment on the video.
  • `nobody` - Only the video owner can comment on the video.
  • `contacts` - Only contacts can comment on the video.

download.description: Set whether the video can be downloaded.

add.description: Set whether other users can add the video to their collections.

Template.description: A template is a saved [Edit](#tocs_edit) than can be loaded and re-used.

TemplateRender.description: Configure the id and optional merge fields to render a template by id.

id.description: The id of the template to render in UUID format.

merge.description: An array of key/value pairs that provides an easy way to create templates with placeholders. The placeholders can be used to find and replace keys with values. For example you can search for the placeholder `{{NAME}}` and replace it with the value `Jane`.

Source.description:
The details of the file to be ingested and any transformations to be applied. Once the source file has been ingested, new renditions can be created from it. The renditions are specified in the **outputs** property.
A rendition is a new version, generated from the source. This can be used to create new sizes and aspect ratios tht serve different purposes within an application.

url.description: The URL of the file to be ingested. The URL must be publicly accessible or include credentials.

callback.description: An optional webhook callback URL used to receive status notifications when sources are uploaded and renditions processed.

Outputs.description: The output renditions and transformations that should be generated from the source file.

renditions.description: The output renditions and transformations that should be generated from the source file.

transcription.description: The transcription settings for the output file.

Rendition.description: A rendition is a new output file that is generated from the source. The rendition can be encoded to a different format and have transformations applied to it such as resizing, cropping, etc...

format.description:
The output format to encode the file to. You can only encode a file to the same type, i.e. a video to a video or an image to an image. You can't encode a video as an image. The following formats are available:
  • `mp4` - mp4 video file (video only)
  • `webm` - webm video file (video only)
  • `mov` - mov video file (video only)
  • `avi` - avi video file (video only)
  • `mkv` - mkv video file (video only)
  • `ogv` - ogv video file (video only)
  • `wmv` - wmv video file (video only)
  • `avif` - avif video file (video only)
  • `gif` - animated gif file (video only)
  • `jpg` - jpg image file (image only)
  • `png` - png image file (image only)
  • `webp` - webp image file (image only)
  • `tif` - tif image file (image only)
  • `mp3` - mp3 audio file (audio only)
  • `wav` - wav audio file (audio only)

fit.description:
Set how the rendition should be scaled and cropped when using a size with an aspect ratio that is different from the source. Fit applies to both videos and images.
  • `crop` (default) - scale the rendition to fill the output area while maintaining the aspect ratio. The rendition will be cropped if it exceeds the bounds of the output.
  • `cover` - stretch the rendition to fill the output without maintaining the aspect ratio.
  • `contain` - fit the entire rendition within the output while maintaining the original aspect ratio.

resolution.description:
The preset output resolution of the video or image. This is a convenience property that sets the width and height based on industry standard resolutions. The following resolutions are available:
  • `preview` - 512px x 288px
  • `mobile` - 640px x 360px
  • `sd` - 1024px x 576px
  • `hd` - 1280px x 720px
  • `fhd` - 1920px x 1080px

quality.description:
Adjust the visual quality of the video or image. The higher the value, the sharper the image quality but the larger file size and slower the encoding process. When specifying quality, the goal is to balance file size vs visual quality.
Quality is a value between 1 and 100 where 1 is fully compressed with low image quality and 100 is close to lossless with high image quality and large file size. Sane values are between 50 and 75. Omitting the quality parameter will result in an asset optimised for encoding speed, file size and visual quality.

fps.description:
Change the frame rate of a video asset.
  • `12` - 12fps
  • `15` - 15fps
  • `24` - 24fps
  • `23.976` - 23.976fps
  • `25` (default) - 25fps
  • `29.97` - 29.97fps
  • `30` - 30fps
  • `48` - 48fps
  • `50` - 50fps
  • `59.94` - 59.94fps
  • `60` - 60fps

keyframeInterval.description: The keyframe interval is useful to optimize playback, seeking and smoother scrubbing in browsers. The value sets the number of frames between a keyframe. The lower the number, the larger the file. Try a value between 10 and 25 for smooth scrubbing.

fixOffset.description:
Attempt to fix audio and video sync issues. This can occur when recording devices, such as smartphones and
 web cams use compression techniques like [Variable Frame Rate](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Variable_frame_rate)
 (VFR) which can cause audio and video to go out of sync. This option will attempt to fix the sync issues.

fixRotation.description: Automatically reset the rotation of the video based on the orientation metadata in the video file. This is useful for videos recorded on smartphones that have orientation metadata that may not work correctly with certain video editing software, including the Shotstack Edit API.

enhance.description: Apply media processing enhancements to the rendition using a third party provider. Currently only Dolby.io audio enhancement is available.

filename.description: A custom name for the generated rendition file. The file extension will be automatically added based on the format of the rendition. If no filename is provided, the rendition ID will be used.

Transcription.description: Generate a transcription of the audio in the video. The transcription can be output as a file in SRT or VTT format.

format.description:
The output format of the transcription file. The following formats are available:
  • `srt` - SRT captions format
  • `vtt` - VTT captions format

Speed.description: Set the playback speed of a video or audio file. Allows you to preserve the pitch of the audio so that it is sped up without sounding too high pitched or too low.

speed.description: Adjust the playback speed of the video clip between 0 (paused) and 10 (10x normal speed) where 1 is normal speed (defaults to 1). Set values less than 1 to slow down the playback speed, i.e. set speed to 0.5 to play back at half speed. Set values greater than 1 to speed up the playback speed, i.e. set speed to 2 to play back at double speed.

preservePitch.description: Set whether to adjust the audio pitch or not. Set to false to make the audio sound higher or lower pitched. By default the pitch is preserved.

Enhancements.description: Enhancements that can be applied to a rendition. Currently only supports the Dolby audio enhancement.

audio.description: An audio enhancement that can be applied to the audio content of the rendition.

AudioEnhancement.description:
An audio enhancement that can be applied to the audio content of a rendition. The following providers are available:
  • DolbyEnhancement

DolbyEnhancement.description: Dolby.io audio enhancement provider. Credentials are required and must be added via the  [dashboard](https://dashboard.shotstack.io/integrations/dolby), not in the request.

provider.description: The enhancement provider to use - set to `dolby` for Dolby.

DolbyEnhancementOptions.description: Options for the Dolby.io audio enhancement provider.

preset.description:
The preset to use for the audio enhancement. The following presets are available:
  • `conference` - Conference
  • `interview` - Interview
  • `lecture` - Lecture
  • `meeting` - Meeting
  • `mobile_phone` - Mobile Phone
  • `music` - Music
  • `podcast` - Podcast
  • `studio` - Studio
  • `voice_over` - Voice Over

Transfer.description: The asset URL to fetch and transfer to a destination.

url.description: The file URL to fetch and transfer.

id.description: An identifier for the asset which must be provided by the client. The identifier does not need to be unique.

destinations.description: Specify the storage locations and hosting services to send the file to.

GeneratedAsset.description: A generated asset is a media asset created by the Create API. You can use native or third party providers to generate video, audio and image files using Generative AI services like text-to-speech and text-to-avatar.

ShotstackGeneratedAsset.description: Generate assets using the native Shotstack provider. Shotstack provides a text-to-speech and a text-to-image service. The Shotstack provider works natively with your existing API key, no additional credentials are required.

provider.description: The name of the provider - set to `shotstack` for Shotstack.

options.description: Options and configuration for the native Shotstack services.

ShotstackGeneratedAssetOptions.description: Generate assets using the native Shotstack provider AI services.

ShotstackTextToSpeechOptions.description: Options for the Shotstack text-to-speech service. Set the text to be converted to speech and choose a voice to set the speaking style. The output will be generated as an MP3 audio file available at the URL returned in the response.

type.description: The type of asset to generate - set to `text-to-speech` for text-to-speech.

text.description: The text to convert to speech.
text.example: This is a text to speech example generated by Shotstack

voice.description:
The voice to use for the text-to-speech conversion. You must pair the correct voice with the correct language. For each language there is a language code that must be set in the `language` property. The voice must be available for the language code or the conversion will fail. i.e. To use the voice `Zeina` for Arabic you must set the `language` to `arb`. QueuedResponse.description: The response received after a [render request](#render-asset) or [template render](#render-template) is submitted. The render task is queued for rendering and a unique render id is returned.

QueuedResponseData.description: The response data returned with the [QueuedResponse](#tocs_queuedresponse).

message.description: Success response message or error details.

id.description: The id of the render task in UUID format.

RenderResponse.description: The response received after a [render status request](#get-render-status) is submitted. The response includes  details about status of a render and the output URL.

RenderResponseData.description: The response data returned with the [RenderResponse](#tocs_renderresponse) including status and URL.

id.description: The id of the render task in UUID format.

owner.description: The owner id of the render task.

status.description:
The status of the render task.
  • `queued` - render is queued waiting to be rendered
  • `fetching` - assets are being fetched
  • `preprocessing` - video assets are being processed for compatibility
  • `rendering` - the asset is being rendered
  • `saving` - the final asset is being saved to storage
  • `done` - the asset is ready to be downloaded
  • `failed` - there was an error rendering the asset

error.description: An error message, only displayed if an error occurred.

duration.description: The output video or audio length in seconds.

renderTime.description: The time taken to render the asset in milliseconds.

url.description: The URL of the final asset. This will only be available if status is done. This is a temporary URL and will be deleted after 24 hours. By default all assets are copied to the Shotstack hosting and CDN destination.

poster.description: The URL of the poster image if requested. This will only be available if status is done.

thumbnail.description: The URL of the thumbnail image if requested. This will only be available if status is done.

data.description: The timeline and output data to be rendered.

created.description: The time the render task was initially queued.

updated.description: The time the render status was last updated.

TemplateResponse.description: The response received after a [template](#create-template) is submitted. The template is saved and a unique template id is returned.

TemplateResponseData.description: The response data returned with the [TemplateResponse](#tocs_templateresponse).

message.description: Success response message or error details.

id.description: The unique id of the template in UUID format.

TemplateDataResponse.description: The template data including the template name and [Edit](#tocs_edit).

TemplateDataResponseData.description: The response data returned with the [TemplateDataResponse](#tocs_templatedataresponse).

id.description: The unique id of the template in UUID format.

owner.description: The owner id of the templates.

TemplateListResponse.description: A list of previously saved templates.

TemplateListResponseData.description: The response data returned with the [TemplateListResponse](#tocs_templatelistresponse).

owner.description: The owner id of the templates.

TemplateListResponseItem.description: The individual template item returned with the  [TemplateListResponseData](#tocs_templatelistresponsedata) templates list.

id.description: The unique id of the template in UUID format.

created.description: The time the template was created.

updated.description: The time the template was last updated.

ProbeResponse.description: The response received after a [probe request](#inspect-media) is submitted. The probe requests returns data from FFprobe formatted as JSON.

response.description: The response from FFprobe in JSON format.

AssetResponse.description: The response returned by the Serve API [get asset](#get-asset) request. Includes details of a hosted video, image, audio file, thumbnail or poster image. The response follows the [json:api](https://jsonapi.org/) specification.

AssetRenderResponse.description: The response returned by the Serve API [get asset by render id](#get-asset-by-render-id) request. The response  is an array of asset resources, including video, image, audio, thumbnail and poster image. The response follows  the [json:api](https://jsonapi.org/) specification.

data.description: An array of asset resources grouped by render id.

AssetResponseData.description: The type of resource (an asset) and attributes of the asset.

type.description: The type of resource, in this case it is an assets.

attributes.description: The asset attributes including render id, url, filename, file size, etc...

AssetResponseAttributes.description: The list of asset attributes and their values.

id.description: The unique id of the hosted asset in UUID format.

owner.description: The owner id of the asset.

region.description: The region the asset is hosted, currently only `au` (Australia).

renderId.description: The original render id that created the asset in UUID format. Multiple assets can share the same render id.

providerId.description: The third party id of an asset transferred to an external provider, i.e. Mux, YouTube or S3. If the provider is Shotstack, the providerID is the same as the asset id.

status.description:
The status of the asset.
  • `importing` - the asset is being copied to the hosting service
  • `ready` - the asset is ready to be served to users
  • `failed` - the asset failed to copy or delete
  • `deleted` - the asset has been deleted

created.description: The time the asset was created.

updated.description: The time the asset status was last updated.

TransferResponse.description: The response returned by the Serve API [transfer asset](#transfer-asset) request. The response includes the ID and transfer status. The response follows the [json:api](https://jsonapi.org/) specification.

TransferResponseData.description: The type of resource (an asset) and the transfer attributes. Returned with [TransferResponse](#tocs_transferresponse).

type.description: The type of resource, in this case it is an asset.

attributes.description: The attributes of the asset transfer including the status.

TransferResponseAttributes.description: The transfer request attributes inlcudling the user specified ID and status. Returned with [TransferResponseData](#tocs_transferresponsedata).

id.description: The user provided ID for the asset

owner.description: The attributes of the asset transfer including the status.

status.description:
The status of the asset transfer.
  • `queued` - the transfer request has been queued
  • `failed` - the transfer request failed

created.description: The time the asset transfer was created.

QueuedSourceResponse.description: The response returned by the Ingest API [fetch source](#fetch-source) request. Includes the id of the source file. The response follows the [json:api](https://jsonapi.org/) specification.

QueuedSourceResponseData.description: The type of resource (a source) and the newly created source id. Returned with [QueuedSourceResponse](#tocs_queuedsourceresponse).

type.description: The type of resource, in this case it is a source.

SourceListResponse.description: A list of all ingested source files fetched or uploaded to a users account.

data.description: An array of ingested source files.

SourceResponse.description: The response returned by the Ingest API [get source](#get-source) request. Includes details of the ingested source file. The response follows the [json:api](https://jsonapi.org/) specification.

SourceResponseData.description: The type of resource (a source), it's id and attributes of the source file.

type.description: The type of resource, in this case it is a source.

attributes.description: The source attributes including its url, status, width, height, duration, etc...

SourceResponseAttributes.description: The id and attributes of the source file.

owner.description: The owner id of the source file.

input.description: The original URL of an ingested source file, where it originated. Only displayed for files ingested using the [fetch source](#fetch-source) endpoint. Not displayed for direct uploads.

source.description: The URL of the source file hosted by Shotstack. The file at the URL can be used by the Edit API. Source file URL's consist of a base URL (AWS bucket), owner id, source id and a file named source. The extension varies depending on the type of file ingested.

status.description:
The status of the source file ingestion task.
  • `queued` - ingestion task is queued waiting to be fetched
  • `importing` - the source file is being downloaded
  • `ready` - the source file has been ingested and stored
  • `failed` - there was an error ingesting the source file
  • `deleted` - the source file has been deleted

outputs.description: The list of outputs generated from the source file. Currently supports renditions which are versions of the source file with different transformations applied.

width.description: The width in pixels of the ingested source file, if a video or image.

height.description: The height in pixels of the ingested source file, if a video or image.

duration.description: The duration in seconds of the ingested source file, if a video or audio file.

fps.description: The frame rate in frames per second of the source file, if a video file.

created.description: The time the ingestion task was initially queued.

updated.description: The time the ingestion status was last updated.

OutputsResponse.description: The list of outputs generated from the source file. Currently supports renditions which are versions of the source file with different transformations applied.

renditions.description: The list of renditions generated from the source file.

RenditionResponseAttributes.description: The id and attributes of the generated rendition file.

status.description:
The status of the rendition transformation task.
  • `waiting` - rendition task is waiting for source file to become available
  • `queued` - rendition task is queued waiting to be processed
  • `processing` - the rendition is being processed
  • `ready` - the rendition is ready to be downloaded
  • `failed` - there was an error creating the rendition
  • `deleted` - the rendition has been deleted

url.description: The URL of the rendition file hosted by Shotstack. The file at the URL can be used by the Edit API. Source file URL's consist of a base URL (AWS bucket), owner id, source id and a file name with the rendition id and extension.

executionTime.description: The time in milliseconds it took to process the rendition.

transformation.description: The transformation applied to the source file to create the rendition.

width.description: The width in pixels of the ingested source file, if a video or image.

height.description: The height in pixels of the ingested source file, if a video or image.

duration.description: The duration in seconds of the ingested source file, if a video or audio file.

fps.description: The frame rate in frames per second of the source file, if a video file.

UploadResponse.description: The response returned by the Ingest API [direct upload](#direct-upload) request. Includes the id of the file and the signed url to send the binary file to. The response follows the [json:api](https://jsonapi.org/) specification.

UploadResponseData.description: The type of resource (an upload), it's id and attributes of the upload request.

type.description: The type of resource, in this case it is an upload.

attributes.description: The upload attributes including the signed URL.

UploadResponseAttributes.description: The id and attributes of the upload file including the signed URL to send the binary file data to.

url.description: The signed URL to use in a PUT request to send the binary file to.

expires.description: The time the upload request will expire. The signed URL will expire after one hour. Upload must complete within one hour.

IngestErrorResponse.description: Error response data for validation and other errors returned by the Ingest API.

IngestErrorResponseData.description: Individual errors returned by the Ingest API.

title.description: A short summary of the error.

detail.description: A detailed description of the error.

GeneratedAssetResponse.description: The response returned by the Create API [generate asset](#generate-asset) and [get generated asset](#get-generated-asset) requests. Includes status and details of the generated asset. The response follows the [json:api](https://jsonapi.org/) specification.

GeneratedAssetResponseData.description: The type of resource (an asset), it's id and attributes of the generated file.

type.description: The type of resource, in this case it is an asset.

attributes.description: The generated asset attributes including its url, status, provider, type, etc...

GeneratedAssetResponseAttributes.description: The id and attributes of the generated asset.

owner.description: The owner id of the asset.

provider.description: The native or third party provider that generated the asset, `shotstack`, `elevenlabs`, `heygen` or `d-id`.

type.description: The type of service used to generate the asset, `text-to-speech` or `text-to-avatar`.

url.description: The URL of the asset file hosted by Shotstack. The file at the URL can be used by the Edit API. Generated asset file URL's consist of a base URL (AWS bucket), owner id, asset id and extension. The extension varies depending on the type of file generated.

status.description:
The status of the asset file generation task.
  • `queued` - generation task is queued waiting to be processed
  • `processing` - the asset is being generated by the provider
  • `saving` - the asset is being saved to Shotstack
  • `done` - the asset has been generated and stored
  • `failed` - there was an error generating the asset

created.description: The time the generate asset task was initially queued.

updated.description: The time the asset status was last updated.

GeneratedAssetErrorResponse.description: Error response data for validation and other errors returned by the Create API.

GeneratedAssetErrorResponseData.description: Individual errors returned by the Create API.

title.description: A short summary of the error.

detail.description: A detailed description of the error.

width.description: The width of the stroke in pixels.

color.description: The stroke color using hexadecimal color notation.

TextAnimation.description: Animation properties for text entrance effects.

preset.description:
The animation preset to apply.
  • `typewriter` - typewriter effect where characters appear one at a time

duration.description: The duration of the animation in seconds.

RichTextBorder.description: Border styling properties for the text bounding box.

width.description: The width of the border in pixels. Must be 0 or greater.

color.description: The border color using hexadecimal color notation.

opacity.description: The opacity of the border where 1 is opaque and 0 is transparent.

radius.description: The border radius in pixels for rounded corners. Must be 0 or greater.

RichTextPadding.description: Padding properties for individual sides of the text bounding box.

RichCaptionFont.description: Font properties for rich captions. Defaults to Roboto.

family.description: The font family name. This must be the Family name embedded in the font, i.e. "Roboto".

size.description: The size of the font in pixels (px). Must be between 1 and 500.

weight.description: The weight of the font. Can be a number (100-900) or a string ('normal', 'bold', etc.). 100 is lightest, 900 is heaviest (boldest).

color.description: The text color using hexadecimal color notation.

opacity.description: The opacity of the text where 1 is opaque and 0 is transparent.

background.description: The background color behind the text using hexadecimal color notation.

RichCaptionStyle.description: Text style properties for rich captions. Same as RichTextStyle but without wordSpacing.

letterSpacing.description: Additional spacing between letters in pixels. Can be negative for tighter spacing.

lineHeight.description: The line height as a multiplier of the font size. Must be between 0 and 10.

size.description: The font size of the active word in pixels. Overrides the scale property when set.

gradient.description: Gradient fill for text instead of solid color.

TiktokDestinationOptions.description: Pass additional options to control how TikTok publishes video.

title.description: A title for the video that will be displayed on TikTok.

privacyLevel.description:
The privacy level for the video. Options are:
  • `public` - video is visible to everyone
  • `friends` - video is visible to friends only
  • `private` - video is only visible to you

disableDuet.description: Disable the Duet feature for this video.

disableStitch.description: Disable the Stitch feature for this video.

TiktokDestination.description: Send videos to TikTok. TikTok credentials are required and added via the [dashboard](https://dashboard.shotstack.io/integrations/tiktok), not in the request.

provider.description: The destination to send video to - set to `tiktok` for TikTok.

AkamaiNetStorageDestinationOptions.description: Pass additional options to control how files are stored in Akamai NetStorage.

cpCode.description: The Content Provider code (CP code) for the NetStorage upload directory.

path.description: A remote directory path/prefix for the file being sent, i.e. `videos` or `customerId/videos`.

filename.description: Use your own filename instead of the default filenames generated by Shotstack. Note: omit the file extension as this will be appended depending on the output format. Also `-poster.jpg` and `-thumb.jpg` will be appended for poster and thumbnail images.

AkamaiNetStorageDestination.description: Send videos and assets to [Akamai NetStorage](https://techdocs.akamai.com/netstorage-usage/docs). Send files to your NetStorage upload directory with a custom path and filename. Akamai credentials are required and added via the [dashboard](https://dashboard.shotstack.io/integrations/akamai-netstorage), not in the request.

provider.description: The destination to send assets to - set to `akamai-netstorage` for Akamai NetStorage.

AzureBlobStorageDestinationOptions.description: Pass additional options to control how files are stored in Azure Blob Storage.

container.description: The Blob container name. The container must exist in the Azure Storage account before files can be sent.

prefix.description: A virtual directory prefix for the blob being sent, i.e. `videos` or `customerId/videos`.

filename.description: Use your own filename instead of the default filenames generated by Shotstack. Note: omit the file extension as this will be appended depending on the output format. Also `-poster.jpg` and `-thumb.jpg` will be appended for poster and thumbnail images.

AzureBlobStorageDestination.description: Send videos and assets to [Azure Blob Storage](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/blobs/). Send files to any container with a custom prefix and filename. Azure credentials are required and added via the [dashboard](https://dashboard.shotstack.io/integrations/azure-blob-storage), not in the request.

provider.description: The destination to send assets to - set to `azure-blob-storage` for Azure Blob Storage.

options.description: Additional Azure Blob Storage configuration options.
